steps.title: Use a common denominator
steps.content:
The common denominator is $(6x-7)(x+1)$. Rewrite each fraction with that denominator.
$$\frac{6}{6x-7}-\frac{1}{x+1}=\frac{6(x+1)}{(6x-7)(x+1)}-\frac{6x-7}{(x+1)(6x-7)}$$

steps.title: Combine the numerators
steps.content:
Subtract the second numerator from the first.
$$6(x+1)-(6x-7)=6x+6-6x+7$$
$$=13$$

steps.title: Write the simplified fraction
steps.content:
Place the simplified numerator over the common denominator.
$$\frac{13}{(x+1)(6x-7)}$$
The answer is D.
